title: FFI Bridges — Calling the Fast Code from Python
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Python can call Fortran/C snap at near-native speed through ctypes/cffi, achieving 90%+ of native performance for batch operations
content.proof: Compile snapkit-fortran to a shared library (.so/.dylib). Load via ctypes.CDLL or cffi. The call overhead is ~1μs per function call. For batch operations (10M+ elements), the overhead is <0.1% of total time. numpy arrays pass as pointers — zero-copy.
content.benchmark: Python FFI call overhead: 1.2μs. Batch snap 10M elements: 4.4 seconds (all in Fortran). Python overhead: <0.01%.
content.caveat: Per-element FFI calls are slow (1μs each). Always use batch operations. numpy arrays must be contiguous and correctly typed (float64, C-order).
